prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: One writer I believe is under-discussed is Gene Wolfe. He is often overlooked in broader literary conversations despite his immense talent and unique approach to science fiction and fantasy. Wolfe's work, such as "The Book of the New Sun" series, is richly layered with complex themes, intricate world-building, and deeply philosophical narratives. What many readers might miss is his ability to blend high literary quality with genre fiction, creating stories that are both intellectually stimulating and imaginatively captivating. His writing demands close attention and often rewards readers with profound insights and unexpected revelations.
